name: Spear-phish to Metasploit C2 (Windows 10)
description: Windows 10 endpoint compromise. Spear-phishing delivered an MSHTA (LOLBin) payload that dropped a timestomped svchost_helper.exe into AppData\Local\Temp, established Run-key persistence, masqueraded as svchost.exe (parent winword.exe, running from Temp), and beaconed to a Metasploit C2 on port 4444.
notes: Evidence is synthetic pre-exported artifacts (Eric Zimmerman / Volatility CSV format). The C2 IP 45.137.21.9 is a synthetic, externally-routable address chosen so NetworkTool's external-IP check fires (documentation ranges like 198.51.100.0/24 are treated as non-globally-reachable by Python's ipaddress module and would not flag).
